concerns: Step 2 is ambiguous about whether existing treble-driven curl3 turbulence (line 60-61 of vert shader) is kept or replaced when adding treble-to-pointSize modulation. Both mappings should coexist per the AC (treble modulates ribbon thickness), but the existing turbulence displacement is also valuable. Clarify that the new treble*pointSize factor is additive to the existing treble behavior.

Low-quality compound mode is under-addressed: 250 points / 100 trailLength = only 2-3 ribbons, which will look broken. The 'minimum sourceCount of 5' mitigation in risks won't help here. Consider a lower trailLength floor (e.g., 40-60) when effectiveCount < 500, or ensure minimum ribbons of 5 by capping trailLength dynamically.

Step 4 only mentions updating uBasePointSize at init, but the .draw() method (line 197) also sets uBasePointSize to 0.08*(1+complexity*0.5). Both sites need coordinated updates or the init value gets immediately overwritten on first frame.

Step 3 references 'fog alpha attenuation from 0.85' but does not specify where this value lives in the current fragment shader — verify the exact current value before changing it to avoid unintended visual regression.

Test thresholds in Step 5 (sourceCount ≤ 30, trailLength ≥ 80) are brittle implementation-detail checks that will break on future tuning. Consider testing ratios or behavioral properties instead (e.g., points-per-ribbon ratio increased vs previous default).
